issues: 1x silhouette reads as indistinct blob — heart shape barely legible at native scale
pixel scatter particles dissolve the outer edge, weakening silhouette on both backgrounds
on light bg the gold/yellow particles lose contrast and fade into off-white
no strong outline or halo to anchor shape against varied backgrounds
reasoning: The heart motif is universally charming and the green+gold palette reads as 'heal/nature' at a glance — good personality signal. At 4x the pixel-art construction is clean and the scattered sparkle particles add energy without feeling aggressive. However the core silhouette test is marginal: at 1x the heart is a fuzzy speck with no readable edge; the particle spray that gives it life at 4x becomes noise at 1x. On the light background the yellow particles nearly disappear, dropping contrast. The sticker test almost passes — a child would recognise 'heart with sparkles' at 4x but struggle at 1x. Charm is present; silhouette clarity is not quite there. Sits solidly in marginal territory.
